problem_text: For integral_1^2 (1/x) dx, use the Trapezoidal Rule error bound |E_T| <= K(b - a)^3 / (12 n^2) to find the smallest number of subintervals n that guarantees an error less than 0.001.

content.meaning: K = max|f''| on [a,b]. For f(x)=1/x, f''(x)=2/x^3 is largest at x=1, so K=2.

content.label: compute f''(x)=2/x^3 and take K = max|f''| = 2 on [1,2]
content.action: compute f''(x)=2/x^3 and take K = max|f''| = 2 on [1,2]
content.purpose: obtain the curvature constant K needed by the error bound

content.label: substitute K=2, b-a=1 into the bound to get 2/(12 n^2) = 1/(6 n^2), then require 1/(6 n^2) < 0.001
content.action: substitute K=2, b-a=1 into the bound to get 2/(12 n^2) = 1/(6 n^2), then require 1/(6 n^2) < 0.001
content.purpose: turn the tolerance into an inequality in n

content.label: solve 6 n^2 > 1000, so n^2 > 166.67 and n > 12.91; round up to the smallest integer n = 13
content.action: solve 6 n^2 > 1000, so n^2 > 166.67 and n > 12.91; round up to the smallest integer n = 13
content.purpose: produce the smallest n that guarantees the tolerance
